manifest.title: Perdura Methodology Re-audit
manifest.description: A finding-by-finding re-audit of Perdura's mathematical, statistical, reliability, prediction, PoF, quality, and decision algorithms against the original 50-finding baseline.

cards.description: Original audit findings whose defect or gap no longer reproduces and whose completion criterion is materially met.
Original findings that still have a corrected primary path but a remaining semantic or diagnostic gap.
Material findings discovered by the fresh audit; the count remains historical even after closure.
Current residual/open risks after completing the re-audit's recommended remediation steps.
Fresh root regression-suite result, with the original audit baseline retained as comparison context.

charts.title: Disposition of the original audit findings
charts.subtitle: All 50 original findings are resolved; partial and unresolved counts are zero.
charts.question: How many original findings are resolved, partial, or unresolved?
charts.rationale: Three exact disposition categories are most directly compared by bar length.

tables.title: Original Critical finding disposition
tables.subtitle: All five primary failure modes now meet their closure criteria, including F005's former optional-path residual.

tables.title: Recommended-step implementation closure
tables.subtitle: The three residual findings and the ALT warning caveat now have direct implementation and test evidence.

tables.title: Complete original-to-current finding reconciliation
tables.subtitle: All 50 original findings plus F051; use the evidence column to trace implementation and tests.

tables.title: Original versus current audit surface
tables.subtitle: Current implementation (c24f96d plus recommended-step remediation) compared with the source state assessed by the original audit.

tables.title: Fresh analytic and adversarial probes
tables.subtitle: All 19 checks pass their independently stated identities or fail-closed reference behavior.

query.description: Loads the original/current audit counts, repository-diff scope, and fresh validation results.
filters: Baseline is commit 5362069^; current implementation is c24f96d plus the recommended-step remediation working tree
metric_definitions: Resolved share = resolved original findings / 50 original findings.
Current risk counts include residual severity for partially resolved original findings plus newly discovered findings.
Root tests are the pyproject-configured tests/ suite; backend tests are reported separately.

query.sql: SELECT * FROM read_csv_auto('docs/audit/re-audit-findings.csv') ORDER BY CAST(SUBSTR(id, 2) AS INTEGER)
query.description: Reconciles every original finding with current code/tests and adds newly discovered findings.
filters: All 50 original findings retained; no finding omitted because its backlog item was marked complete
metric_definitions: Resolved = the original defect/gap no longer reproduces and its definition of done is materially met.
Partially resolved = the primary defect is corrected but a specified semantic or diagnostic gap remains.
New = not present in the original register; F051 was discovered by the re-audit and resolved in this follow-up.

query.description: Counts resolved, partially resolved, and unresolved dispositions across the original 50 findings.
metric_definitions: Share denominator is the 50 original findings; the new F051 is reported separately.

sources.label: Fresh analytic and adversarial probe results

query.sql: SELECT * FROM read_csv_auto('docs/audit/re-audit-probe-results.csv') ORDER BY finding, probe
query.description: Loads direct dimensional, analytic, monotonicity, censoring, and fail-closed probes generated by docs/audit/re-audit-probes.py.
filters: Deterministic probes except seeded tests already recorded by the main test suite
metric_definitions: Pass means the current output agrees with an independently stated identity or reference behavior.

query.description: Compares the current calculation surface with the inventory frozen by the original audit.
filters: Core reliability modules and backend calculation routers
metric_definitions: Current public-symbol count includes the 73 MIL-HDBK implementation symbols exported through the public facade.
Python line count covers src/reliability and gui/backend/routers.

query.query: Original research register plus fresh NIST interval-censoring/degradation and official scikit-learn Lasso review on 2026-07-11
query.description: Supports the methodological distinction between observed censoring intervals and projection uncertainty, and the sparse-regression optimality assessment.
filters: Primary publications or official technical documentation

blocks.body:
## Technical summary

**All 50 original audit findings now meet their closure criteria, and the newly discovered F051 is also resolved.** Partial, unresolved, and current residual-severity counts are zero. The follow-up separated degradation projection uncertainty from censoring likelihood, added KKT and support-stability diagnostics to sparse regression, replaced FTA/RBD probability defaults with typed fail-closed contracts, removed non-finite ALT optimizer arithmetic, and made the reconciliation executable as a release gate.

**Overall assessment: release gate passed within disclosed scope.** This means the recorded counterexamples, evidence links, and regression contracts pass; it is not regulatory certification or proof of model adequacy for every application.

The final validation baseline is 1,306 passing root tests with 0 warnings, 241 passing backend tests, 284 focused MIL/VITA core tests, 15 focused standards-API tests, and a passed production frontend build.

## One hundred percent of the original findings now meet their closure criteria

The chart counts disposition, not effort: a finding is resolved only when the original behavior no longer reproduces and the implementation has corresponding code/test evidence. F051 remains outside the original denominator because it was discovered during the re-audit, but it is resolved and retained in the full reconciliation.

## The five original Critical failure modes now meet full closure criteria

Mission-rate units, RDT boundary search, exact FTA semantics, and cumulative step-stress exposure match direct identities. Degradation uses one likelihood contribution per unit: genuine interval likelihood only for observed crossing intervals, point inputs for projected crossings, and right-censoring for non-crossing units. Delta-method projection bounds are retained as explicitly display-only uncertainty.

## Every recommended remediation step is implemented

**F051 now fails closed** through typed direct-probability and eight-family distribution contracts at both request and computation boundaries. **F005 now separates projection uncertainty from censoring:** only observed inspection brackets enter interval likelihood. **F048 now requires coefficient-change and KKT optimality checks** and reports a ten-times-stricter tolerance support comparison. **The ALT warning path uses an excluded finite penalty** so Powell interpolation cannot perform infinity arithmetic. **The audit register is enforced by tests** for identity retention, severity preservation, evidence paths, closure state, and fresh probes.

## Every original finding is retained in the comparison

The table below is the audit diff: original severity, present status, implementation mechanism, evidence, and residual limitation. Broad method additions were credited only when the original decision risk was removed or made explicit; disclosed scope limitations remain visible even for resolved findings.

## Closure was tested on a substantially larger calculation surface

The re-audit did not compare equal-sized snapshots. Public calculation symbols increased by 26%, endpoint count by 6%, Python calculation code by 41%, and root regression coverage by 37%. The new inventory includes the full MIL-HDBK-217F/VITA implementation behind its public facade. This expansion raises—not lowers—the need for domain-specific validation despite the improved finding count.

## Standards conformance is now explicit and MIL/VITA coverage is materially complete

F022 is resolved through machine-readable conformance tiers, clause/example disclosures, and screening labels for incomplete commercial methods. The focused current run passed 284 MIL-HDBK-217F/VITA core tests and 15 standards-API tests. MIL-HDBK-217F Notice 2 maps Sections 5–23, 217 Appendix A recipes, and Appendix B mechanisms; ANSI/VITA 51.1 activates only when selected. This is verified implementation evidence within documented scope—not contractual certification of a prediction or independent approval of the disclosed Appendix F engineering interpretation.

## Independent probes confirm the algorithm changes, including the former residual edges

The 19-row probe harness repeats the original counterexamples using current public calculations and direct identities: FPMH dimensional conversion, the monotone chi-square RDT boundary, shared-event Boolean probability, cumulative exposure, extreme-tail hazard, target conservation, criticality, memorylessness, matrix rank, and bound ordering. Its F005, F048, and F051 negative probes now pass as release-gated closure evidence.

## Scope, definitions, and methodology

The comparison baseline is the source state immediately before remediation commit `5362069`; the assessed implementation is `c24f96d` plus this recommended-step remediation. The original re-audit diff spans 190 files, 31,387 insertions, and 6,054 deletions; this follow-up changes the affected algorithms, contracts, tests, probes, and report on top of that state. The audit traced every original finding to current code and tests, reran direct probes, reviewed the expanded MIL/VITA evidence, scanned calculation paths for silent defaults and overstated labels, and reran core, backend, focused standards, and frontend validation.

**Resolved** means the original behavior no longer reproduces and the completion criterion is materially met. **Partially resolved** remains a gated status but has zero rows. **New** identifies provenance outside the original register; it does not imply F051 remains open. Current severity is based on present—not historical—exposure and defaults.

## The implemented closures follow standard statistical semantics

[NIST defines interval censoring](https://www.itl.nist.gov/div898/handbook/apr/section1/apr131.htm) as knowing that an event occurred between observation times, while its [degradation guidance](https://www.itl.nist.gov/div898/handbook/apr/section4/apr423.htm) describes fitted crossing times as projections and recommends reality checks against actual failures. Perdura now enforces that distinction: projection intervals are display-only and only observed inspection brackets enter interval likelihood.

For F048, the official [scikit-learn Lasso contract](https://scikit-learn.org/stable/modules/generated/sklearn.linear_model.Lasso.html) follows small coordinate updates with an optimality test. Perdura now evaluates the implemented penalized objective's KKT conditions and separately reports whether a ten-times-stricter tolerance refit preserves the active support.

## Limitations, uncertainty, and robustness

This is a change-focused source-and-algorithm re-audit, not a clean-room reimplementation, regulatory certification, or exhaustive proof over all input combinations. Every original finding received a current trace, but unchanged Tier-2 paths were sampled rather than rederived line by line. Passing tests demonstrate specified behavior; they do not prove model adequacy for a user's population, mechanism, loss function, or contractual standard.

The former ALT SciPy warning path now passes warning-as-error fitter and refitted-bootstrap tests; invalid objective regions use a large finite sentinel and sentinel-valued optimizer results are ineligible. Controlled MIL/VITA documents were available locally; other commercial standards remain screening-only because licensed clause/table parity is absent. The A/V Appendix F equation uses a documented dimensional repair that should receive independent domain review before contractual use.

## Recommended next steps — execution complete

1. **F051 complete:** typed, finite, domain-constrained FTA/RBD probability inputs cover all eight supported families; adversarial public-router tests prove there is no default substitution.
2. **F005 complete:** extrapolated bounds are display-only projection uncertainty; interval likelihood is reserved for observed inspection bounds.
3. **F048 complete:** lasso and elastic net require objective KKT optimality and report deterministic support stability under a stricter tolerance.
4. **ALT warning hardening complete:** invalid regions no longer feed infinities to scalar interpolation, and the affected tests run with RuntimeWarning promoted to error.
5. **Release gate complete:** tests preserve all 50 original identities and severities, require zero partial/open rows, validate evidence paths and scope disclosures, and rerun all 19 probes.

## Further questions

- Is exchangeable beta-factor common cause sufficient for Perdura's intended users, or are partial-group, load-sharing, and conditional-environment models required?
- Who will independently review the A/V Appendix F dimensional repair and the standards evidence before contractual use?
- Which small-sample, censored, boundary, weak-identification, and sparse-selection regimes should be added to the uncertainty coverage matrix next?
- Should degradation projection uncertainty eventually move from first-order delta bounds to a joint hierarchical degradation/life model?

summary.validation_assessment: Release gate passed within disclosed scope

original_critical.resolution: System roll-up now converts FPMH to failures/hour exactly once before MTBF and reliability calculations.
The planner brackets the monotone chi-square boundary and uses integer binary search for the maximum passing failure count.
Exact cut-set probability now uses a reduced Shannon BDD; the minimal-cut bound is separately named and a state-limit breach raises instead of changing methods.
original_critical.residual_or_limitation: Exact evaluation remains subject to an explicit state limit.

original_critical.resolution: Step-stress equivalent time sums accelerated exposure from every completed step and validates the active stress interval.

The likelihood now uses one contribution per unit: true F(U)-F(L) terms only for observed inspection crossings, projected crossings as point inputs, and S(C) for non-crossing units. Delta-method projection intervals are separately labeled display-only uncertainty.
original_critical.residual_or_limitation: Projection intervals remain first-order delta-method diagnostics rather than a hierarchical measurement-error model; that scope is explicit and does not alter censoring likelihood.

remediation_closures.resolution: The likelihood now uses one contribution per unit: true F(U)-F(L) terms only for observed inspection crossings, projected crossings as point inputs, and S(C) for non-crossing units. Delta-method projection intervals are separately labeled display-only uncertainty.
remediation_closures.residual_or_limitation: Projection intervals remain first-order delta-method diagnostics rather than a hierarchical measurement-error model; that scope is explicit and does not alter censoring likelihood.

remediation_closures.resolution: Shared diagnostics require optimizer success, finite parameters/objective/gradient, acceptable projected gradient, and expose boundary contact before fit eligibility. ALT invalid regions now use an excluded finite penalty so Powell interpolation never performs infinity arithmetic.
remediation_closures.residual_or_limitation: Warning-as-error ALT fitter and parametric-bootstrap tests pass; no optimizer RuntimeWarning remains in the validated suite.

remediation_closures.resolution: Lasso and elastic net validate hyperparameters, standardize predictors, require both scaled coefficient-change and penalized-objective KKT criteria, and report objective, KKT residual/tolerance, and active support.
remediation_closures.residual_or_limitation: Support stability is a deterministic ten-times-stricter tolerance refit, not bootstrap selection-frequency inference; the method and comparison diagnostics are returned explicitly.

remediation_closures.resolution: RBD components and fault-tree basic events now use typed, finite, domain-constrained probability/distribution inputs. Missing fields, unknown families, invalid parameters, invalid times, and out-of-range direct probabilities fail request validation; compute boundaries revalidate and never substitute defaults.
remediation_closures.residual_or_limitation: None reproduced across all eight supported distribution families and adversarial public-router payloads.

findings.resolution: System roll-up now converts FPMH to failures/hour exactly once before MTBF and reliability calculations.
findings.residual_or_limitation: None reproduced.

findings.resolution: The planner brackets the monotone chi-square boundary and uses integer binary search for the maximum passing failure count.
findings.residual_or_limitation: None reproduced.

findings.resolution: Exact cut-set probability now uses a reduced Shannon BDD; the minimal-cut bound is separately named and a state-limit breach raises instead of changing methods.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Exact evaluation remains subject to an explicit state limit.

findings.resolution: Step-stress equivalent time sums accelerated exposure from every completed step and validates the active stress interval.
findings.residual_or_limitation: None reproduced.

findings.resolution: The likelihood now uses one contribution per unit: true F(U)-F(L) terms only for observed inspection crossings, projected crossings as point inputs, and S(C) for non-crossing units. Delta-method projection intervals are separately labeled display-only uncertainty.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Projection intervals remain first-order delta-method diagnostics rather than a hierarchical measurement-error model; that scope is explicit and does not alter censoring likelihood.

findings.resolution: Part-construction failures now raise MissionCalculationError with part, phase, class, and original exception context.
findings.residual_or_limitation: None reproduced.

findings.resolution: Undefined AICc returns infinity and makes the candidate ineligible for AICc ranking.
findings.residual_or_limitation: BIC and predictive validation retain their own assumptions.

findings.resolution: Hazard and cumulative hazard use log-PDF/log-survival evaluation, preserving extreme-tail identities.
findings.residual_or_limitation: None reproduced for implemented distributions.

findings.resolution: Shared diagnostics require optimizer success, finite parameters/objective/gradient, acceptable projected gradient, and expose boundary contact before fit eligibility. ALT invalid regions now use an excluded finite penalty so Powell interpolation never performs infinity arithmetic.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Warning-as-error ALT fitter and parametric-bootstrap tests pass; no optimizer RuntimeWarning remains in the validated suite.

findings.resolution: Standard and ALT likelihoods now use distribution logpdf/logsf contributions rather than probability floors.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Clipping remains only in plotting or delta-transform guardrails, not the audited likelihood paths.

findings.resolution: Scalar profile-likelihood and refitted parametric-bootstrap intervals are available; mixture and competing-risk reliability can use refitted bootstrap intervals; Wald output is labeled approximate.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Published seeded coverage is a 50-replicate regular complete-sample smoke study, not broad coverage certification.

findings.resolution: KS inference uses a parametric bootstrap that refits the model on every successful replicate and reports calibration details.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Bootstrap validity remains conditional on the fitted family and simulation design.

findings.resolution: Chi-square bins are merged to the expected-count threshold, inference is declined without residual degrees of freedom, and p-values use refitted bootstrap calibration.
findings.residual_or_limitation: None reproduced.

findings.resolution: AGREE weights are normalized after complexity/importance weighting so the series allocation conserves the target.
findings.residual_or_limitation: The implementation documents its target-conserving interpretation of AGREE weighting.

findings.resolution: RBD importance now reports failure criticality using component and system unreliability, with textbook Birnbaum, RAW, and RRW checks.
findings.residual_or_limitation: None reproduced.

findings.resolution: PAND, XOR, and NOT are rejected by the static coherent solver instead of being reduced to incorrect AND/independent-event semantics.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Order-aware dynamic gates still require a separate state-space implementation.

findings.resolution: FTA and RBD now expose an exact latent-event beta-factor common-cause model and disclose the active independence/dependency assumptions.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Only exchangeable all-members shocks are modeled; MGL/alpha-factor partial groups, load sharing, conditional environment, and beta uncertainty remain out of scope.

findings.resolution: Post-burn-in mean residual life uses the closed-form incomplete-gamma infinite tail; the exponential memoryless identity passes.
findings.residual_or_limitation: None reproduced.

findings.resolution: Non-increasing hazards return run-to-failure; wear-out searches expand the range and never label an unqualified boundary minimum as an optimum.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Long-run renewal assumptions remain explicit.

findings.resolution: MCF input requires observation ends or explicit event/censor records; events tied to observation end remain events.
findings.residual_or_limitation: None reproduced.

findings.resolution: MCF uncertainty uses Nelson increments with subject-cluster robust influence variance, log intervals or cluster bootstrap, and effective-risk counts.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Sparse right-tail estimates remain visibly flagged.

findings.resolution: Every named prediction and derating method returns an edition, scope, exclusions, clause coverage, example status, and conformance tier; MIL-HDBK-217F and VITA 51.1 have controlled coverage and parity suites.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Telcordia, 217Plus, FIDES, NSWC, EPRD/NPRD, and named derating tables remain explicitly screening-only.

findings.resolution: Legacy JHEDI/SHERPA/ATHEANA/MERMOS calculators are labeled and routed as screening heuristics; SPAR-H implements PSFs, dependency, and beta uncertainty.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Screening aliases remain for project/API migration and carry warnings.

findings.resolution: Capability returns an explicit stability assessment/status and withholds the capability decision unless stability is demonstrated.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Indices remain visible as diagnostic estimates when the decision is withheld.

findings.resolution: OLS checks matrix rank, identifies aliased terms, and blocks coefficient inference for non-identifiable designs.
findings.residual_or_limitation: None reproduced.

findings.resolution: All PoF endpoints validate finite physical domains and units, return mechanism/validity contracts, and optionally propagate input uncertainty.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Intervals quantify selected input uncertainty, not population reliability or unmodeled mechanism uncertainty.

findings.resolution: Rank-biserial correlation is now 2U_A/(n_A n_B)-1 and the API states the group-A-minus-group-B direction.
findings.residual_or_limitation: None reproduced.

findings.resolution: Repeated-measures ANOVA reports Mauchly, Greenhouse-Geisser, Huynh-Feldt, and lower-bound corrections; mixed analysis uses pooled REML covariance with explicit approximations/fallback.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Mixed-model denominator degrees of freedom remain an explicitly labeled approximation.

findings.resolution: Classical Gage R&R rejects incompatible designs; REML supports unbalanced crossed and nested designs and reports component intervals, rank, convergence, and boundaries.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Observed-information intervals can remain weak near variance boundaries and are flagged.

findings.resolution: SPC separates traceable Phase-I screening from frozen Phase-II baselines and rejects unsupported subgroup sizes instead of clamping constants.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Phase-I exclusions still require assignable-cause review rather than automatic deletion.

findings.resolution: Nonnormal capability compares empirical, Harrell-Davis, fitted-family, and Box-Cox approaches with bootstrap intervals and tail-sufficiency diagnostics.
findings.residual_or_limitation: The fitted-family bootstrap is conditional on the originally selected family and omits selection uncertainty.

findings.resolution: Two-level analysis requires exactly two finite levels and reports mappings, rank, aliases, and estimability; other design classes dispatch to dedicated models.
findings.residual_or_limitation: None reproduced.

findings.resolution: Plackett-Burman generation advertises only verified cyclic/Sylvester constructions, supports up to 63 factors, and checks X'X=N I.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Intermediate requests advance to the next supported run order and disclose capacity.

findings.resolution: Categoricals use fold-fitted one-hot encoding for sklearn estimators and native train-only category/bin handling for CHAID; unknown-category behavior is disclosed.
findings.residual_or_limitation: One-hot unknown categories become an all-zero group as stated.

findings.resolution: SVM/KNN/MLP scaling is inside the training pipeline; convergence is captured; stratified, grouped, and time splits plus calibration/imbalance metrics are selectable.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Comparison is model evaluation, not nested hyperparameter optimization; no tuning claim is made.

findings.resolution: ALT reports transformed-space interpolation/extrapolation, convex-hull position, leverage, common-shape diagnostics, and optional refitted bootstrap use-life intervals.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Non-rejection of common shape is not proof of a common physical failure mechanism.

findings.resolution: Single/dual-stress designs are rank- and condition-checked in model coordinates, harmful directions are constrained, and remote use points receive leverage and uncertainty diagnostics.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Physical direction constraints are conditional on the selected model parameterization.

findings.resolution: Mixture and competing-risk fits use multiple starts and expose effective weight, component separation, collapse, boundary, stability, identifiability, and simpler-model recommendations.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Weakly identifiable fits remain available only as explicitly ineligible diagnostics.

findings.resolution: Weibayes response contract v2 enforces sf_lower <= sf <= sf_upper and preserves separately named legacy curves.
findings.residual_or_limitation: No backward-compatibility ambiguity remains in v2 fields.

findings.resolution: Weibayes supports beta-range sensitivity envelopes and Bayesian beta propagation under an explicit prior.
findings.residual_or_limitation: The Bayesian result remains conditional on the chosen truncated-normal beta prior.

findings.resolution: Duane instantaneous MTBF is withheld outside 0 <= alpha < 1 with a Crow-AMSAA/change-point recommendation.
findings.residual_or_limitation: The cumulative Duane regression remains visible as descriptive output.

findings.resolution: A finite-horizon Kijima-II virtual-age simulation models imperfect maintenance and uncertainty; older renewal-rate forecasts explicitly disclose their steady-state approximation.
findings.residual_or_limitation: The simulation is not a capacity-constrained repair-shop or general semi-Markov inventory model.

findings.resolution: Spares analysis now offers negative-binomial overdispersion and finite-horizon renewal/replenishment simulation with stochastic lead times and common shocks.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Multi-echelon logistics, repair capacity, priority, and condemnation yield remain out of scope.

findings.resolution: Nevada returns retain fractional weights and use grouped interval-censored likelihood; units still in service are right-censored and forecast parameter intervals are exposed.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Reporting-lag, future count variation, and model-selection uncertainty remain excluded and disclosed.

findings.resolution: Fault-tree Monte Carlo reports Wilson intervals, exact one-sided zero-event bounds, and the 1/n resolution limit.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Simulation remains separately labeled from exact evaluation.

findings.resolution: FTA and acyclic RBD exact probabilities use memoized reduced BDDs with explicit state diagnostics; bounded path enumeration is display-only.
findings.residual_or_limitation: State limits and acyclic-RBD scope are explicit; dynamic/repair loops require state-space methods.

findings.resolution: PoF now compares Paris/Walker/Forman crack growth, Goodman/Soderberg/Gerber mean stress, and linear/nonlinear cumulative damage, with separate model and input sensitivity.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Most mechanism laws still depend on analyst-supplied calibrated coefficients and do not quantify model-form probabilities.

findings.resolution: Lasso and elastic net validate hyperparameters, standardize predictors, require both scaled coefficient-change and penalized-objective KKT criteria, and report objective, KKT residual/tolerance, and active support.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Support stability is a deterministic ten-times-stricter tolerance refit, not bootstrap selection-frequency inference; the method and comparison diagnostics are returned explicitly.

findings.resolution: The DOE contract now includes randomization, blocking, alias/rank diagnostics, coefficient power, lack-of-fit, response-surface, mixture, and constrained designs.
findings.residual_or_limitation: Computer-aided optimal designs and broader constrained-region families remain future extensions, not hidden assumptions.

findings.resolution: CTMC assumptions are explicit; Erlang phase-type dwell alternatives preserve means/destination probabilities; transition-rate CVs can be propagated by seeded Monte Carlo.
findings.residual_or_limitation: The option is bounded Erlang phase type, not a general semi-Markov solver or data-based rate posterior.

findings.resolution: RBD components and fault-tree basic events now use typed, finite, domain-constrained probability/distribution inputs. Missing fields, unknown families, invalid parameters, invalid times, and out-of-range direct probabilities fail request validation; compute boundaries revalidate and never substitute defaults.
findings.residual_or_limitation: None reproduced across all eight supported distribution families and adversarial public-router payloads.

scope.definition: Top-level public calculation classes/functions; current count includes 73 implementation symbols exported through the MIL-HDBK facade.
Decorated calculation routes across the same API-domain inventory.
Core reliability modules plus backend calculation routers.
Passing tests in the pyproject-configured root test suite.
Calculation modules excluding package/version shims and counting the MIL implementation behind its public facade once.

probes.basis: Convert FPMH to failures/hour exactly once.
Invalid parts must not contribute a silent zero rate.

probes.probe: Maximum passing exponential RDT failure count

probes.basis: P(A and any B_i)=P(A)[1-product P(not B_i)].
Sum every completed step under its acceleration factor.

probes.probe: One likelihood contribution per degradation unit
probes.basis: Observed intervals use F(U)-F(L) and non-crossing units use S(C).

probes.probe: Projection uncertainty kept separate from censoring
probes.current: 0 projection intervals in likelihood; 2 display-only intervals available
probes.basis: A confidence interval around a fitted crossing time is not an observed failure interval.
For eta=1 beta=2 h(t)=2t and H(t)=t^2.
Product of series allocations equals target.
Birnbaum times component unreliability divided by system unreliability.
Latent shared and independent terms reconstruct each marginal.
Weibull beta <=1 has no improving finite age-replacement optimum.

probes.current: Rejected with rank 2 of 3 and aliased x_copy

probes.basis: Every group-A observation exceeds every group-B observation.
Bounds are named by survival ordinate.

probes.current: converged=true; KKT residual 8.60e-15 <= 2.28e-4; stricter-tolerance support stable
probes.reference: Objective/KKT check and explicit active-set stability
probes.basis: Coordinate-change stopping is followed by a KKT check and a stricter-tolerance support comparison.

probes.probe: Malformed FTA/RBD probability inputs fail closed
probes.current: RBD float_parsing error; FTA greater_than error
probes.basis: Invalid probability inputs are rejected and never replaced by plausible defaults.

query.query: Original research register plus fresh NIST interval-censoring/degradation and official scikit-learn Lasso review on 2026-07-11
